title: How to Use Media Picker in GoHighLevel — Manage Images Fast
description: Learn how to use GoHighLevel's Media Picker to manage images fast. Upload once, use everywhere. Perfect for agencies managing multiple client accounts.
html_content:
Managing images across your GoHighLevel account shouldn't be a nightmare. If you're running an agency—or managing multiple client accounts—you know how quickly digital assets can spiral out of control. Forms, surveys, quizzes, landing pages, and QR codes all need consistent, on-brand imagery. That's where GoHighLevel's Media Picker comes in.

The Media Picker is a centralized image management system that lets you browse, upload, and reuse assets instantly across every part of your account. No more hunting through folders. No more uploading the same logo ten times. Just streamlined workflows and brand consistency at scale.

In this guide, I'll walk you through exactly how to use it—and how it can cut your content creation time in half. Ready to try it yourself? Get a free 30-day trial of GoHighLevel here—that's double the standard trial period.

📋 In This Guide

  • What Is the Media Picker in GoHighLevel?
  • How to Access Media Storage and the Media Picker
  • Uploading Images to Your Media Library
  • Using Media Picker in Forms, Surveys, and Quizzes
  • Reusing Assets Across Multiple Elements
  • Best Practices for Digital Asset Management

What Is the Media Picker in GoHighLevel?

The Media Picker is GoHighLevel's unified image selection system. It connects directly to your Media Storage—a centralized repository for all your digital assets. Instead of uploading images individually to each form, survey, or page, you upload once and access everywhere.

Think of it like this: your Media Storage is your filing cabinet. The Media Picker is the drawer you open whenever you need something from that cabinet. Both work together seamlessly.

GoHighLevel recently migrated from its older Media/File Asset Picker to the Global Media Picker for better consistency and access to newer features. This means regardless of whether you're building a form, a quiz, or embedding images in a landing page, you're using the same intuitive interface.

The benefits?

  • Speed: No re-uploading. Browse existing assets instantly.
  • Brand consistency: Use the same approved images every time.
  • Storage efficiency: One image, unlimited uses.
  • Team collaboration: Share assets across client accounts without duplicates.

How to Access Media Storage and the Media Picker

Getting to your Media Storage is straightforward.

Step 1: Log in to GoHighLevel
Open your GoHighLevel dashboard and navigate to the main menu.

Step 2: Find Media Center
Look for the Media Center or Media Storage option in your account settings. The exact location may vary slightly depending on your GoHighLevel version, but it's typically found under:

  • Settings → Media Storage
  • Or directly accessible from the main navigation menu

Step 3: Review Your Media Library
Once inside, you'll see all previously uploaded assets organized and ready to use. The Global Media Picker ensures this same view appears whenever you need to select an image—whether you're building a form, creating a quiz, or designing a landing page.

💡 Pro Tip

The Media Picker is context-aware. When you click "Select Image" in a form field or quiz question, the Media Picker appears with a search function already active. You don't need to navigate to Media Storage separately—it comes to you.

Uploading Images to Your Media Library

Before you can use images in forms and quizzes, they need to live in your Media Storage. Here's how to add them.

Option 1: Upload from Media Center

  1. Go to Media Storage in your GoHighLevel account.
  2. Click the Upload or Add New button.
  3. Select image files from your computer. GoHighLevel supports common formats like PNG, JPG, GIF, and WebP.
  4. Click Upload and wait for the confirmation.

Option 2: Upload During Form/Quiz Creation

  1. While editing a form or quiz, click the image element.
  2. Select Upload New Image from the Media Picker.
  3. Choose your file—it will automatically be added to your Media Storage for future use.

Both methods work, but uploading during creation is faster if you're building multiple assets at once.

Using Media Picker in Forms, Surveys, and Quizzes

This is where the Media Picker earns its keep. Let's walk through a practical example.

Adding Images to a Form:

  1. Open your form builder in GoHighLevel.
  2. Add a new field and select an image-based element (like an image field or banner).
  3. Click Select Image. The Media Picker modal opens.
  4. Browse your existing images or use the search bar to find a specific asset.
  5. Click the image to select it. It's now embedded in your form.

Adding Images to a Quiz:

  1. Navigate to your quiz builder.
  2. Create a new question and select the option to include an image.
  3. Click Select Image to open the Media Picker.
  4. Choose your image from the library or upload a new one.
  5. Save the question.

Adding Images to Surveys:
The process is identical—any element that accepts images will trigger the Media Picker when you interact with it.

The consistency across forms, surveys, and quizzes is one of the best features of the Global Media Picker. No more learning different interfaces for different elements.

Reusing Assets Across Multiple Elements

This is where you'll save serious time. Once an image is in your Media Storage, you can reuse it infinitely.

Real-world scenario: You've uploaded your company logo to Media Storage. Now you need it in:

  • Your lead magnet form
  • A follow-up survey
  • A product quiz
  • Your landing page header
  • Email signatures (if integrated)

Instead of uploading five times, you simply select the logo from the Media Picker in each place. It's the same file, so any edits you make to the original automatically reflect everywhere it's used.

This approach is particularly valuable for agencies managing multiple client accounts. Your client's brand assets live in one place, accessible instantly across all their forms, funnels, and campaigns.

Best Practices for Digital Asset Management

Having a Media Picker is one thing. Using it strategically is another. Here's how to maximize it.

1. Use Descriptive Filenames
When uploading images, name them clearly. Instead of "image_1.jpg," use "logo-blue-2024.png" or "hero-banner-homepage.jpg." This makes searching the Media Picker faster.

2. Create a Folder Structure (if available)
If GoHighLevel supports folder organization in your plan, organize assets by client or asset type. This prevents your Media Storage from becoming a digital junk drawer.

3. Use High-Quality, Optimized Images
Before uploading, compress images to reduce file size without sacrificing quality. Faster loads = better user experience and faster form submissions.

4. Maintain a Brand Asset Library
For each client, keep approved logos, colors, and images in one accessible location. This ensures brand consistency and makes it easy to onboard team members.

5. Regularly Audit and Clean Up
Delete old versions and unused assets periodically. This keeps your Media Storage lean and makes browsing faster.

6. Leverage Media Picker in Rich Text Editors
GoHighLevel's Media Picker integrates with rich text editors too. When you're writing form descriptions, quiz instructions, or email content, use the Media Picker to insert images directly without leaving the editor.

💡 Pro Tip

Archive (don't delete) outdated campaign images. You might need them later for A/B testing or client reporting. Most plans offer plenty of storage—use it strategically.

The Media Picker and Media Storage integration is a game-changer for agencies running campaigns at scale. It removes friction from asset management and ensures every form, quiz, and survey maintains brand consistency. Once you start using it, you'll wonder how you ever managed images any other way.
